weather.description: Mild & long daylight, near 19h of sun

quickFacts: Stockholm spans 14 islands — walking across bridges is half the fun
Tap water is excellent — no need to buy bottled
Almost entirely cashless — card or Swish for everything
Fika (coffee + pastry break) is a cultural institution
foodScene: Fika is sacred — kanelbullar (cinnamon buns) at a cosy café are mandatory. Swedish meatballs with lingonberry at a traditional restaurant, not IKEA. Toast Skagen (shrimp on toast) at waterfront restaurants. Street food at Hornstulls Marknad.

places.name: Gamla Stan (Old Town)
places.caption: Medieval island centre
places.description: Narrow cobblestone alleys, ochre-coloured buildings, and the smallest statue in the world. The Royal Palace changing of the guard is free at noon.

places.name: Vasa Museum
places.caption: 17th-century warship
places.description: A nearly intact warship that sank on its maiden voyage in 1628, salvaged 333 years later. The most visited museum in Scandinavia for a reason.

places.name: Fotografiska
places.caption: Photography museum & bar
places.description: World-class photography exhibitions in a converted customs house. The top-floor restaurant has panoramic views and excellent cocktails.

places.name: Södermalm
places.caption: Hipster island
places.description: Vintage shops, indie coffee roasters, and the best sunset viewpoint at Monteliusvägen. SoFo district for boutiques and brunch.

places.name: Djurgården Island
places.caption: Museum & nature island
places.description: Walk or take the ferry. Skansen outdoor museum, Vasa Museum, ABBA Museum, and peaceful forests — all on one island.

places.name: Stockholm Archipelago
places.caption: 30,000 islands
places.description: Take a day ferry to Vaxholm, Grinda, or Sandhamn. Pine-covered islands, swimming from rocks, and seafood at waterside restaurants.

places.name: Monteliusvägen
places.caption: Best sunset walk
places.description: A short clifftop path in Södermalm with sweeping views over Gamla Stan and Lake Mälaren. Bring a blanket and a bottle of wine.

places.name: ABBA The Museum
places.caption: Interactive pop shrine
places.description: Sing, dance, and virtually perform on stage with ABBA holograms. Even non-fans leave grinning. Book timed tickets online.

places.name: Skansen Open-Air Museum
places.caption: Sweden in miniature
places.description: Historic buildings from across Sweden, Nordic animals (moose, bears), and traditional crafts. The oldest open-air museum in the world (1891).

places.name: Day Trip: Drottningholm Palace
places.caption: Royal residence
places.description: The Swedish royal family's home, a UNESCO site accessible by scenic boat ride. The 18th-century court theatre still stages performances.

places.name: Royal Palace
places.caption: Baroque residence
places.description: 608 rooms — one of Europe's largest palaces. Watch the daily changing of the guard. Royal Apartments and Treasury open to visitors.

places.name: SkyView (Ericsson Globe)
places.caption: Gondola on a sphere
places.description: Glass gondola on the outside of the world's largest spherical building. Quick ride to the top for city panoramas.
